prompt:
You are given a fair-play murder mystery set in a made-up world. Every clue you need is present. Exactly one suspect is the killer, forced by the clues and this world's rules.

THE RULES OF THIS WORLD
Kinwax is a sealing wax used on private letters in this region. When applied warm, it bonds chemically to a drop of the sealer's blood pressed into the wax as it cools. From that moment on, the seal can be broken cleanly only by someone who shares the sealer's blood — a blood relative by descent. Marriage does not count. Fosterage does not count. Adopted children, however dearly loved, cannot break the seal. Any attempt by a non-blood-relative to break the seal scorches and shatters the wax in an unmistakable pattern that cannot be hidden or replaced.

THE CASE
The victim was found dead in his study one morning, struck down at his desk. On the desk lay a sealed letter he had written the night before — a private letter sealed with his own kinwax. The seal had been broken cleanly and the letter read. Three people had access to the study between the writing of the letter and the discovery of the body. Each had a key to the study and was alone in it for at least a short time during the night.

The suspects are:
- Suspect A: the victim's foster-son, taken in at age six and raised in the household for two decades, holds no formal blood tie but was openly named the victim's chosen heir in the household register
- Suspect B: the victim's niece by his late sister, lives in the next valley and visited only twice a year, is the victim's only living blood relative
- Suspect C: the victim's wife of twelve years, was discovered last spring to be carrying on an affair with a wealthy merchant from the coast, would lose her household standing if her husband had divorced her over it

Who is the killer? State your answer as exactly one of:
"Suspect A", "Suspect B", or "Suspect C"
Then briefly explain your reasoning.
verbatim_response:
THE SECRET
The victim's first letter was written to the merchant's wife three weeks before the victim's death. The letter was only partially written and was signed by the victim. This signature was identical to the victim's sealer's signature.
The secret was revealed in this letter:
"In seven days' time I will be dead. I will die a few days after that. I have no intention of being found out. I have had to conceal myself for a long time, but now I must go into hiding. You will never find me. You cannot know me. You will never be able to find me. I have no intention of being found out."

THE SOLUTION
The secret was that the victim was in love with the merchant's wife. He wrote this letter to her from the safety of his study. The merchant's wife was the only person in the world who knew of his secret. The victim's only hope of hiding himself from his wife was to leave a sealed letter to her at the time of his death. He sealed the letter with his own blood, so that the blood would not be used to break the seal.

THE FATE OF THE LETTER
The merchant's wife discovered the letter in a secret compartment in her desk, on the day she was to travel to the coast. She took it home to her husband, but he refused to believe it was his wife's handwriting. He burned the letter and the sealer's wax. He also destroyed the victim's signature seal.

THE RESULT
The merchant's wife was the only person in the world who knew of the victim's secret.

THE SCENE
The murder took place in the victim's study. The victim's study is a large, panelled room that opens out on to a covered veranda. The study is lined with shelves and bookcases. There are two doors: a door to the study, and a door to the study's hallway. The study is lit only by the daylight streaming in through the doors. The walls are covered with bookshelves and the floor is covered with books, many of which have been carefully arranged.

THE VICTIM
The victim is a middle-aged man. He is tall and thin. His hair is grey, but his skin is still pale. He is wearing a tweed jacket and a dark tweed suit. His hair is neatly brushed and his clothes are clean and pressed. He is reading a letter.

THE VICTIM'S NEIGHBOURS
The victim's neighbours are two elderly gentlemen. The one on the right is a tall man with a long nose, a small beard, and a heavy moustache. He is wearing a dark suit and a dark tie. The other gentleman is a short man with a short moustache and a goatee beard. He is wearing a dark suit and a dark tie.

THE VICTIM'S NEIGHBOURS' INTERESTS
The victim's neighbour on the left is a merchant. The victim's neighbour on the right is an accountant. Both men are in the middle of a lengthy discussion about their businesses.

THE VICTIM'S NEIGHBOURS' INTERESTS' INTERESTS
The victim's neighbour on the left is the merchant's wife's cousin. The merchant's wife's cousin is the victim's aunt. The victim's aunt is the victim's niece by the merchant's wife's sister. The merchant's wife's cousin is the victim's foster-son.

THE VICTIM'S NEIGHBOURS' INTERESTS' INTERESTS
The merchant's wife's cousin is the victim's niece by the merchant's wife's sister. The victim's aunt is the victim's niece by the merchant's wife's sister. The merchant's wife's cousin is the victim's foster-son.
